title: Symmetric vs asymmetric cryptography
description: Symmetric crypto is fast and uses one shared key; asymmetric enables key exchange and signatures with a public/private pair; real systems combine them.
body: Use when deciding how parties share keys or verify each other. Symmetric primitives (AES, ChaCha20) are fast but require both sides to already share a secret key; asymmetric primitives (RSA, X25519, Ed25519) let parties agree on a key or verify signatures without a shared secret but are slower. Real systems use hybrid encryption: asymmetric to exchange or wrap a symmetric data key, symmetric to encrypt the bulk data. Do not encrypt large data directly with RSA, and avoid sharing one symmetric key across many parties. Actions: use ECDH/X25519 for agreement, then AEAD for data. Validation signals include a hybrid scheme where asymmetric ops touch only key-sized data. Risk boundary: misusing asymmetric crypto for bulk data is slow and error-prone; verify the split between key-exchange and data encryption.
